description: Centralize the story security-configuration response contract and canonical wire messages used by request-time authorization code.
acceptance-criteria: FailureResponse.ForSecurityConfiguration returns the full DMS ProblemDetails envelope with type urn:ed-fi:api:system:configuration:security, title Security Configuration Error, status 500, detail A security configuration problem was detected. The request cannot be authorized., validationErrors {}, an errors array populated from the supplied security-configuration messages, and correlationId.

Canonical helpers or constants exist for the four story messages: missing security metadata, empty matched strategy list, unknown strategy implementations, and custom-view basis property/path failure.

The no-strategies helper accepts the CMS action name, deterministic matched resource claim URI list, and matched resource claim Name value; it does not use the API client's assigned claim-set name as claimName.
The no-strategies helper formats the canonical phrase against resource URIs ['{uri1}', '{uri2}'] and never falls back to the older resource ['{resourceName}'] wording.
The unknown-strategy helper aggregates one or more unavailable strategy names in deterministic order.

When request-time authorization discovers multiple deterministic security-configuration failures before selecting a response, the supplied errors array preserves configured or order-of-discovery order.

Existing scenario-specific security-configuration diagnostics from relationship, namespace, invalid namespace prefix, SQL Server parameter-cap, and invalid AUTH1 payload paths remain available and are not remapped unless they exactly match a story canonical scenario.
The story checks are request-time response checks and do not introduce or depend on startup validation beyond the existing DMS-1091 startup tasks.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Response/FailureResponse.cs plus any Core.External or backend message helper files for the exact ProblemDetails shape and message text.

description: Update ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddleware to classify claim-set metadata outcomes according to the story 500-versus-403 boundary.
acceptance-criteria: When claim-set lookup succeeds but returns an empty claim-set catalog, the request returns the canonical missing-security-metadata 500 response.
When claim-set lookup succeeds but the authenticated client's assigned claim-set name is absent from the returned catalog, the request returns the canonical missing-security-metadata 500 response.

When the assigned claim set exists but has zero resource claims, no matching resource claim, or no matching action claim for the requested resource/action, the request remains an authorization-denied 403.

When one or more matching resource/action claims exist but the effective authorization strategy list is empty, the request returns the canonical no-strategies security-configuration 500 response using the deterministic matched resource claim URI list and matched resource claim Name.
Matching resource/action claim selection is deterministic and does not turn duplicate or multi-match authorization metadata into a generic 500 response.

Claim-set provider transport failures, cache/provider exceptions, deserialization failures, and timeouts remain generic system failures with diagnostic logging instead of missing-security-metadata responses.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Middleware/ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddleware.cs for the missing-metadata, denied-action, and empty-strategy branches.

description: Carry request-surface and CMS action metadata through authorization resolution so story responses and logs can distinguish normal CRUD/read actions from change-query ReadChanges actions.
acceptance-criteria: Normal resource and descriptor routes continue to map POST, GET, PUT, and DELETE to CMS actions Create, Read, Update, and Delete.
Existing change-query /deletes and /keyChanges routes, if implemented in the DMS request pipeline, map to the CMS action ReadChanges before strategy lookup and no-strategies message formatting.
Change-query route detection happens before the generic GET-to-Read mapping so supported /deletes and /keyChanges requests cannot accidentally format Read in security-configuration responses.

If no change-query route is currently implemented in the DMS request pipeline, the implementation leaves a small regression test or code assertion proving the current routes cannot accidentally format Read instead of ReadChanges for a supported change-query surface.
The selected action value is reused consistently in ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddleware, backend authorization request models, no-strategies responses, and structured diagnostic logs.
steps-to-verify: Review path parsing and authorization action selection in src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Middleware for resource, descriptor, and any change-query routes.

description: Normalize relational backend strategy-name classification and security-configuration message mapping for unknown, custom-view-convention, and known-but-staged authorization strategies.
acceptance-criteria: Relational authorization planning classifies built-in strategy names before applying the custom-view {BasisResource}With... convention.
A non-built-in strategy whose basis resource or descriptor resolves for the requested operation is treated as custom view-based metadata, not as an unknown strategy.

A strategy name that does not match the convention, whose convention basis cannot be resolved, or whose resolved custom-view strategy is invalid for the requested authorization surface returns the canonical unknown-strategy security-configuration 500 message.

Unknown or unavailable strategy names use the story wire message Could not find authorization strategy implementations for the following strategy names: '{strategyName1}', '{strategyName2}'.; richer relational planner details remain in logs or internal diagnostics, not the response body for this canonical scenario.
Known valid strategies that are intentionally staged behind a sibling story continue to return explicit 501 Not Implemented guardrails where those guardrails already own the surface.

ReadChanges strategies unsupported for the change-query authorization surface return the canonical unknown-strategy security-configuration 500 message instead of a staged 501 when change-query authorization exists.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationStrategyClassifier.cs and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs for classification and message formatting.

description: Standardize any already surfaced custom view-based authorization basis path failures as story security-configuration ProblemDetails without implementing custom view authorization planning or execution.
acceptance-criteria: If an existing classifier, staged planner, or shared failure mapper can already produce a custom view strategy failure where the basis resource or descriptor resolves but no usable authorization join/path is available, that failure maps to the canonical custom-view basis-property security-configuration message.

If no current request-time path can produce a resolved-basis/no-join custom view failure, story does not add new custom view path planning solely to create that scenario; convention-resolved custom view strategies continue to follow the existing staged 501 or unsupported-surface behavior owned by DMS-1062 and DMS-1063.

For mapped custom view path failures, the wire message formats targetEntityName and basisEntityName as schema-qualified resource names such as edfi.CourseTranscript and edfi.Student, including descriptors as schema-qualified descriptor resource names.

For mapped custom view path failures, the propertyName placeholder is chosen deterministically from the missing basis identity/readable property; if only a surrogate DocumentId path failure is available, the logs include the DocumentId anchor and the wire response uses the closest readable basis identity property.
This work does not add custom view SQL execution, custom auth view DDL, new custom view join/path planning, or CRUD/GET-many custom view strategy semantics owned by DMS-1062 and DMS-1063.
steps-to-verify: Review RelationshipAuthorizationStrategyClassifier, relational planner/repository mapping code, and any custom-view-related failure mapper for mapped custom-view security-configuration failures and absence of new custom view execution SQL.

description: Ensure every relational request-time authorization surface that can emit a security-configuration failure uses FailureResponse.ForSecurityConfiguration and the story status/body contract.
acceptance-criteria: GET-many, GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE resource paths map backend Query/Get/Upsert/Update/Delete security-configuration result variants to HTTP 500 application/problem+json responses from FailureResponse.ForSecurityConfiguration.

Descriptor read, write, and delete paths that pass through relational authorization use the same security-configuration result mapping and do not fall back to generic system 500 bodies for metadata misconfiguration.
Change-query /deletes and /keyChanges paths that pass through request-time authorization use the same security-configuration result mapping for ReadChanges metadata misconfiguration.

Existing request-time authorization misconfiguration paths that currently return generic 500 bodies or inconsistent security-configuration bodies are converted to the shared formatter, while unrelated non-authorization generic 500 paths are left unchanged.

The implementation scope stays on the relational backend-redesign path plus shared Core middleware/result mapping; Old.Postgresql-specific authorization helpers are not changed unless they already pass through a shared path being corrected.
No story response path is implemented as a startup validation, startup cache warm-up failure, or DMS-1091 startup task behavior change.
The response body never includes raw JWTs, secrets, provider exception text, SQL text, or stack traces.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Handler and descriptor-related backend handlers for all *FailureSecurityConfiguration result mappings.

description: Add structured error logging for every story security-configuration response path using local logging conventions.
acceptance-criteria: Each security-configuration response path emits one error-level structured log entry when the response is produced, without duplicate logs for the same failure as it flows from backend result to HTTP response.

Middleware-originated failures log tenant, correlationId, HTTP method, route/resource path, request surface, CMS action, assigned claim set, matched resource claim URI or URIs when available, configured strategy name or names when available, and the stable wire error message.

Backend/planner-originated failures log tenant and correlationId when available, request surface, HTTP method, route/resource path when available, CMS action, resource full name, configured strategy names and configured indexes when available, provider or planner failure kind, and the stable wire error message.
Custom-view failures also log target resource full name, basis resource or descriptor full name, missing/readable property, and any resolved physical table/column path when available.
Structured diagnostic fields do not include raw JWTs, secrets, SQL text, or stack traces, and provider exception text remains out of the wire response.
steps-to-verify: Review the middleware, handler, and backend repository/planner paths that create FailureResponse.ForSecurityConfiguration responses for structured LogError calls.

description: Add focused unit tests for story response formatting, middleware classification, action mapping, strategy classification, handler mapping, and structured diagnostics.
acceptance-criteria: FailureResponse unit tests assert the exact story envelope, dynamic correlationId presence, validationErrors {}, and errors array behavior.

ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddleware unit tests cover empty claim-set catalog, missing assigned claim set, existing claim set with zero resource claims, no matching resource claim, no matching action claim, empty matched strategy list, and claim-set provider exception boundaries.
Middleware tests assert canonical no-strategies wording with action, deterministic resource claim URI list, and matched resource claim Name, and assert 403 remains for ordinary permission denial.

Existing tests that assert the older no-strategies wording with resource ['{resourceName}'] or the API client's assigned claim-set name are updated to assert the canonical resource URIs ['{uri1}', '{uri2}'] and matched resource claim Name wording.

Strategy classifier and backend mapper tests cover built-in, custom-view-convention resolved, custom-view-convention unresolved, non-convention unknown, known-but-staged 501, and ReadChanges unsupported-strategy behavior where applicable.
Handler tests cover Query, Get, Upsert, Update, and Delete security-configuration result mapping to HTTP 500 application/problem+json.
Representative log assertions verify structured diagnostic fields for a middleware no-strategies failure and a backend planner/provider security-configuration failure.

description: Add API-level integration coverage for representative relational story responses through the in-process DMS pipeline.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL API-level integration tests in src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration cover at least one resource read security-configuration failure and one write-side security-configuration failure through the in-process DMS pipeline.
PostgreSQL API-level integration tests cover at least one descriptor authorization security-configuration failure if descriptors pass through relational authorization.
PostgreSQL API-level integration tests cover change-query ReadChanges security-configuration behavior if /deletes or /keyChanges authorization exists in the DMS pipeline.
SQL Server API-level integration tests cover the same representative HTTP mappings where the relational authorization path is supported and local MSSQL test prerequisites are available.
API-level integration tests assert the stable ProblemDetails members exactly and assert correlationId is present and non-empty rather than fixed.
API-level integration tests assert that ordinary no matching resource/action permission cases remain 403 and are not accidentally converted to story 500 responses.

API-level integration tests do not require an exhaustive verb-by-surface matrix; they use focused cases that exercise resource read/write, descriptor, and change-query path families where those surfaces exist.
steps-to-verify: Review skipped/conditional integration tests to confirm any absent descriptor or change-query authorization surface is explicitly gated rather than silently ignored.

description: Add backend provider integration coverage for planner, executor, and provider-originated security-configuration failures before Core HTTP response mapping.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL backend integration tests in src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration cover representative query, single-record read, and write-side security-configuration failures that originate below the Core HTTP pipeline.

SQL Server backend integration tests in src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration cover the same representative security-configuration result propagation where the relational authorization path is supported.

Provider-specific failure cases, including SQL Server authorization parameter-cap failures where applicable, return typed Query/Get/Upsert/Update/Delete security-configuration results with stable error entries.
Backend provider integration tests assert typed backend results and error messages, not HTTP ProblemDetails members that belong to Core handler/API-level tests.
Backend provider integration coverage stays on the backend-redesign relational providers and does not add Old.Postgresql-specific authorization parity work.
steps-to-verify: Review backend provider integration assertions to confirm they stop at typed security-configuration results and leave HTTP envelope assertions to Core/API-level tests.

description: Add focused relational-backend E2E coverage using real CMS claim-set metadata for the externally observable story cases.
acceptance-criteria: A relational-backend E2E scenario with a matched resource/action claim and an empty authorization strategy list returns the canonical no-strategies 500 ProblemDetails response.
A relational-backend E2E scenario with an unknown strategy name or an invalid custom-view strategy name returns the canonical unknown-strategy 500 ProblemDetails response.

A relational-backend E2E scenario where claim-set metadata lookup succeeds but no usable metadata exists for the authenticated client returns the canonical missing-security-metadata 500 ProblemDetails response.

E2E assertions validate type, title, status, detail, validationErrors {}, errors, and a present non-empty correlationId; they do not assert a fixed generated correlationId unless the test intentionally sends the configured correlation-id header.
The scenarios use CMS setup data and the relational dms-local stack; they do not depend on direct in-memory claim-set providers.
steps-to-verify: If a focused security-configuration category is not added, run the specific generated Reqnroll scenario names from src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization using the same build-dms.ps1 relational command.
Inspect dms-local container logs for SecurityConfigurationFailure entries matching the E2E correlationId values.

description: Run final formatting and targeted verification for the story implementation without changing unrelated production or test behavior.
acceptance-criteria: All touched DMS source and test files are formatted with csharpier.
Core unit tests, backend planner/backend unit tests, focused API-level integration tests, and focused backend provider integration tests for story pass.
Relational-backend E2E security-configuration scenarios pass through build-dms.ps1 with the relational environment file.

A final source review confirms story did not implement custom view SQL execution, alter unrelated generic 500 paths, or update Old.Postgresql-specific authorization helpers except through shared paths.
A final source review confirms 403 authorization-denied behavior remains for ordinary no matching resource/action permission cases.
